rationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)

This is the second consecutive no_changes for this bead (prior attempt
20260428T222708-3b6ff345 reached the same conclusion). I re-verified the
upstream / DDx state and concur with that prior analysis: the bead is a
multi-PR epic, not a single bounded execute-bead pass, and it should be
split before another implementation attempt.

## What is verified done

1. All six upstream routing-plan beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` are
   `closed`:
     agent-0dafc7f0, agent-191a74f9, agent-1f46cf22,
     agent-53f38d95, agent-bab52778, agent-dfabb10b.

2. Their closing commits are reachable from the tag DDx already pins:
   `cli/go.mod` → `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.
   `git merge-base --is-ancestor <SHA> v0.9.23` succeeds for the
   closing commits of every routing-plan bead checked
   (db26108, b645280, 0bc2bf4, b85e90e). So the upstream binary
   surface AC #1 wants is already on the consumer's classpath; no
   `go.mod` bump is even required.

## Why this still cannot be a single-pass commit

The bead's AC enumerates 10 items spanning D1–D5 plus follow-ups:

  - D1+D2: rip out DDx-side tier-ladder synthesis from the default
    `ddx agent run` / `ddx work` / `ddx agent execute-bead` paths so
    each issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call —
    touches `workerDispatchAdapter`, `execute_bead_loop.go`,
    `agent_cmd.go`, `profile_ladder.go`, `serviceconfig.go`.
  - D3: insert a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before
    `beadStore.Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~352`.
  - D4: deliberate breaking config change — `default_harness` becomes
    a hard error at load; `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides`
    move to opt-in-only with one-time warnings, requires schema
    deletion + migration test + CHANGELOG breaking-change note.
  - D5: CLI override surface — flag plumbing + per-flag e2e tests
    covering harness-only, model-only, harness+model conflict, and
    profile+harness conflict.
  - AC #6: Playwright drain-queue golden matching the user's real
    endpoint config + a "no gemini + non-gemini-model fallback"
    assertion.
  - AC #7: search-and-delete pass for any DDx-side
    allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring re-implementation, plus a
    dead-code detector wired into CI to catch orphans of
    `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef`.
  - AC #8: file follow-up beads for D6 (route-status / providers UI)
    and reaffirm `ddx-05b4cc9d` independence.
  - AC #9: link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream docs and write
    DDx CHANGELOG entry.
  - AC #10: net-new regression integration test that reproduces the
    19-burn drain-queue failure mode with the user's exact config —
    requires fixture work (gemini binary presence, minimax override)
    that is itself a sub-bead.

Conservatively: 1–3k LOC across ~10 Go files plus frontend Playwright
fixtures plus CI wiring plus tracker mutations. Crucially D4 is a
deliberate breaking change to user-facing config schema; landing it
without explicit user alignment risks breaking real `.ddx.yml` files
in the wild. The bead description itself describes the work as "one
DDx PR" but the AC list is the union of nine independent
deliverables.

execute-bead's contract is to satisfy AC deterministically, not to
make architecture or breaking-change decisions across nine
sub-deliverables in one pass. A partial commit would fail most AC
items; a sprawling commit would couple breaking schema changes to
mechanical refactors and frontend fixtures, which is exactly the
shape of PR the bead's own sequencing tries to avoid.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

Split this bead into the nine smaller beads listed in the prior
attempt's rationale (`.ddx/executions/20260428T222708-3b6ff345/result.json`,
section "What a follow-up attempt needs"):

  1. routing-bump (trivial; AC #1 — though arguably already
     satisfied by v0.9.23)
  2. routing-default-path (D1 + D2)
  3. routing-preflight-gate (D3)
  4. routing-config-deprecation (D4 — breaking, needs explicit
     user sign-off in its own bead description)
  5. routing-overrides-e2e (D5 + AC #5)
  6. routing-drain-regression (AC #10)
  7. routing-cleanup (AC #7 dead-code detector)
  8. routing-frontend-goldens (AC #6)
  9. routing-followups (AC #8)

Recommendation: reopen this bead with helix-evolve to perform the
split, keep ddx-fdd3ea36 as the epic tracker, and queue beads 2 and 3
first — they unblock G1/G2/G3 with the smallest reasonable PR.

The reduced-scope language in the bead's own description ("DDx-side
tracker only", "blocks on a single upstream point release") confirms
this was meant as an EPIC, not a deliverable. Two consecutive
execute-bead passes have now reached the same conclusion; further
re-execution at higher model tiers will not change the outcome —
the blocker is bead shape, not capability.

rationale: Bead: ddx-fdd3ea36 — Adopt ddx-agent routing point release (DDx-side consumer)
Attempt: 20260429T003007-e136e75e (no-op)

(a) WHAT IS DONE
================
- AC #1 is satisfied by prior work on this branch:
  * cli/go.mod pins github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent at v0.9.23 (verified).
  * CHANGELOG.md (commit 7d50a792) documents the consumed upstream
    capabilities and cites each upstream commit + bead. The changelog
    explicitly notes that upstream shipped routing capabilities incrementally
    across v0.9.10..v0.9.24 rather than as a single tag.
  * All six upstream beads labeled `routing-plan` in
    ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl are CLOSED:
      agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware tiebreak)
      agent-191a74f9 (first-class default + local + standard profiles)
      agent-1f46cf22 (routing reference docs)
      agent-53f38d95 (public RouteDecision.Candidates trace)
      agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)
      agent-dfabb10b (typed ErrHarnessModelIncompatible / ErrProfilePinConflict)
  * The needed public types are present in the pinned module at
    /home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!document!driven!d!x/agent@v0.9.23/routing_errors.go:
      ErrHarnessModelIncompatible, ErrProfilePinConflict, ErrNoProfileCandidate,
      ErrUnknownProfile, ErrNoLiveProvider, DecisionWithCandidates interface
      with RouteCandidates() []RouteCandidate.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING THIS ATTEMPT
=================================
The bead is an epic-tracker, not a single deliverable. AC #2 through #10
collectively require — in one PR with no compensating paths retained — all of:

  D1. workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker rewrite + Drain-button parity.
  D2. Removal of default-path tier-ladder synthesis from
      ddx agent run / ddx agent execute-bead / ddx work; ResolveProfileLadder
      and ResolveTierModelRef gated behind --escalate only.
  D3. Preflight gate inserted before beadStore.Claim in execute_bead_loop.go
      (the bead points at line 352, which has already drifted — the actual
      Claim site is now line 341; the loop is 1052 lines and has substantial
      surrounding dispatch state to thread through).
  D4. Hard-error breaking change for `agent.routing.default_harness`;
      one-time warnings + default-path ignore for `profile_ladders` and
      `model_overrides`. Schema deletion in
      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json. Config-load test fixtures.
  D5. CLI surface parity for ddx agent run (no flags / --profile / --harness /
      --model / combos), ddx work, ddx agent execute-bead.

  Plus tests demanded by the AC:
  - AC #2 integration test reproducing the original 19-burn drain failure
    against the user's real config shape.
  - AC #3 worker-level failure record assertion with seeded pathological route
    via an upstream test seam that does not currently exist on the DDx side.
  - AC #5 four end-to-end override-behavior tests (incl. cost-tiebreak
    seeded-providers test).
  - AC #6 Playwright golden for the drain-queue UI path
    (cli/internal/server/frontend); fixture must mirror user's real
    endpoint config.
  - AC #7 dead-code detector wired into CI catching orphaned ladder helpers.
  - AC #10 regression-guard integration test that fails before this work and
    succeeds after.

  Plus the directive in AC #7 — "Search DDx for code that re-implements any
  of: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping,
  provider cost scoring. Must be absent. If found, PR fails review." —
  requires a deliberate audit + removal pass spanning at least:
      cli/internal/agent/harness_config.go
      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go
      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder_test.go
      cli/internal/config/config.go
      cli/internal/config/types.go
      cli/internal/config/routing_ladder_test.go
      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json
      cli/internal/server/workers.go
      cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go
      cli/cmd/agent_run_profile_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (1052 lines)
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_runwithconfig_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_resolvers_e2e_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_pin_propagation_test.go

This is a multi-thousand-line, multi-subsystem refactor with a hard
breaking-change config migration and a new Playwright golden. It cannot be
landed responsibly in a single execute-bead pass without a much narrower
sub-bead breakdown. Attempting it as one commit risks: half-finished tier-
ladder removal that leaves the default path in an undefined state; a config
schema break that hard-errors existing user configs without the migration
warnings actually firing; or merging a Playwright golden whose fixture
silently passes because the underlying dispatch path was not fully rewired.

The previous attempt already correctly scoped this bead to AC #1 only and
documented the upstream prerequisite. Doing more here without
decomposition would either produce a bad commit or violate
DOWITYTD on a bead whose own description acknowledges it is a
"DDx-side tracker only" for an upstream-driven epic.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT NEEDS
==================================
Decompose ddx-fdd3ea36 into independently shippable sub-beads, each
small enough to land in one execute-bead pass with focused tests:

  ddx-<new>-D1: workerDispatchAdapter + Drain button — empty-spec defaults to
                Profile:"default"; no other synthesis. Unit test +
                dashboard wiring. Closes AC #2 partially.

  ddx-<new>-D2a: Default path stops calling ResolveTierModelRef /
                 ResolveProfileLadder. Add --escalate flag that re-enables
                 them. Tests assert call graph. Closes AC #2 fully.

  ddx-<new>-D3: Preflight gate before Claim using upstream's typed
                ErrHarnessModelIncompatible. Targeted test using upstream
                test seam (file the upstream test-seam ask as a separate
                ddx-agent bead if missing). Closes AC #3.

  ddx-<new>-D4: Config breaking-change migration. Hard-error on
                default_harness; warn-and-ignore on profile_ladders/
                model_overrides; schema update; migration fixture tests;
                CHANGELOG migration guide. Closes AC #4.

  ddx-<new>-D5: CLI flag-surface parity tests for ddx agent run / work /
                execute-bead with the four override permutations from AC #5.
                Closes AC #5.

  ddx-<new>-Audit: Audit pass for AC #7 (compensating logic removal +
                   dead-code orphan detector in CI). Closes AC #7.

  ddx-<new>-Regression: AC #10 regression-guard integration test that
                        reproduces the 19-burn drain bug; AC #2's
                        integration sibling. Closes AC #10 (and validates
                        D1+D2+D3 together).

  ddx-<new>-Playwright: AC #6 frontend golden against the rewired Drain
                        path. Last in sequence — depends on D1+D2+D3 +
                        Regression. Closes AC #6.

  ddx-<new>-Docs: Cross-link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream profile
                  catalog + override precedence + best-provider contract
                  docs (AC #9 second half — CHANGELOG half is already in
                  CHANGELOG.md from the prior commit).

  ddx-<new>-D6-followup: Decision-trace consumers in route-status /
                         providers UI — explicitly out of scope per the
                         bead description but should be filed now so the
                         tracking exists (bead description's own AC #8
                         requires this).

ddx-fdd3ea36 should remain open as the umbrella tracker until all
sub-beads close, at which point AC #2..#10 can be re-verified end-to-end
in one final review pass.

NO CODE CHANGES IN THIS ATTEMPT.

rationale: Bead: ddx-fdd3ea36 — Adopt ddx-agent routing point release (DDx-side consumer epic)

Disposition: no_changes — this is an epic/tracker bead whose work has been
deliberately decomposed into nine child beads. Some are closed; four remain
open. The tracker cannot close until those four close. There is no
implementation work that belongs at the epic level itself.

================================================================================
What is done
================================================================================

Upstream prerequisite (AC #1):
  All six routing-plan beads in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/ are closed and merged
  into tagged ddx-agent releases (v0.9.10..v0.9.24):
    agent-191a74f9  profiles (default/local/standard)         closed
    agent-bab52778  supported-models allow-list + gating      closed
    agent-dfabb10b  ErrHarnessModelIncompatible / PinConflict closed
    agent-53f38d95  public RouteDecision.Candidates           closed
    agent-0dafc7f0  CostUSDPer1kTokens + cost tiebreak        closed
    agent-1f46cf22  routing documentation                     closed
  cli/go.mod pins github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23, which contains
  every routing-plan commit (verified via `git tag --contains <sha>` against
  closing_commit_sha for each upstream bead).

DDx-side child beads already closed:
  ddx-967e9802  routing-bump (go.mod pin + CHANGELOG)         closed
  ddx-755f5881  routing-default-path (D1 + D2)                closed   → AC #2
  ddx-98e6e9ef  routing-preflight-gate (D3)                   closed   → AC #3
  ddx-dd4c423d  routing-drain-regression test                 closed   → AC #10

Code evidence the closed work is in tree:
  cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go:20-21
    test seams verify ResolveProfileLadder/ResolveTierModelRef are NOT called
    on the default path (per ddx-755f5881 AC #3)
  cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:84-120
    DispatchWorker normalizes empty input to {Profile: "default"} only
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:360-392
    routing preflight gate runs BEFORE Claim at line 394 (D3, ddx-98e6e9ef)
  cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1683-1684
    --escalate / --override-model flags exist; tier ladder + model_overrides
    are off by default (consistent with D2/D4 design)
  cli/internal/config/types.go:230-234
    migration note: agent.routing.default_harness was REMOVED in ddx-87fb72c2

================================================================================
What is open (blocks tracker close)
================================================================================

ddx-87fb72c2  routing-config-deprecation                      open  → AC #4
              hard error on default_harness; ladders/overrides opt-in only;
              CHANGELOG migration entry. (Note: the field-removal half appears
              landed per types.go:230-234 — verify what residual scope this
              bead still owns before executing it.)

ddx-2589e256  routing-overrides-e2e                           open  → AC #5
              --harness/--model/--profile flag plumbing parity tests for
              ResolveRoute(Profile, Harness, Model) shape; cost-tiebreak e2e;
              typed-error surfacing for incompatible combos.

ddx-950321ef  routing-frontend-goldens                        open  → AC #6
              Playwright drain-queue fixtures matching the real failing
              config; Drain button → live-endpoint-backed harness;
              never gemini+non-gemini-model; never 19-burn.

ddx-653f6ac9  routing-cleanup                                 open  → AC #7
              dead-code detector for orphaned compensating logic;
              remove residual allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring code
              that re-implements upstream behavior.

ddx-a0cf398b  routing-followups                               open  → AC #8
              file the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer
              bead; close out D6/D7 housekeeping.

================================================================================
Why this bead does not get implementation in this pass
================================================================================

1. The epic's AC is the union of its children's AC. Every remaining AC item
   (#4, #5, #6, #7, #8, plus the documentation half of #9) is the contract of
   a specific open child bead. Implementing them here would either duplicate
   work that the child bead will redo, or steal scope that another worker
   may already be planning to claim.

2. The bead description's "Sequencing" section explicitly orders:
     1. upstream lands (done)
     2. go.mod bump (done — ddx-967e9802)
     3. D1+D2+D3+D5 land together (D1+D2=ddx-755f5881 done; D3=ddx-98e6e9ef
        done; D5=ddx-2589e256 OPEN)
     4. D4 (ddx-87fb72c2) lands with or right after the above (OPEN)
     5. follow-ups (ddx-a0cf398b, ddx-950321ef, ddx-653f6ac9) (OPEN)
   The tracker is meant to close once steps 3–5 complete via their child
   beads, not to carry independent code.

3. Attempting any of {D4, override e2e, playwright goldens, dead-code sweep}
   in this single execute-bead pass would land a sprawling cross-cutting
   commit that violates the per-child-bead acceptance contracts and is
   hard to review. Per CLAUDE.md "DOWITYTD" and the merge-policy memory,
   the right action is to execute the children individually.

================================================================================
What a follow-up attempt should do
================================================================================

This tracker should NOT be re-queued for direct execution. Instead:

  a) Execute the four open child beads in their natural order:
        ddx-87fb72c2 (D4 — config deprecation residue + CHANGELOG)
        ddx-2589e256 (D5 — override flag e2e)
        ddx-950321ef (D6-equivalent — Playwright goldens)
        ddx-653f6ac9 (cleanup / dead-code sweep)
        ddx-a0cf398b (file follow-up beads)
     Each closes one or two AC items on this tracker.

  b) When all five close, re-open this tracker briefly to:
        - verify every AC item maps to a closed child or in-tree evidence
        - update DDx CHANGELOG (AC #9) if not already done by ddx-967e9802
        - close ddx-fdd3ea36 with a closing-comment that points at each
          child bead's closing commit.

That close step is documentation/bookkeeping — a single small commit, well
suited to a future execute-bead pass once the children are green.

No code changes were made in this pass. Workspace is clean.

NO CHANGES RATIONALE
====================

The implementation is already present at the base revision (9cc24280) of this
worktree. It was landed by an earlier attempt in commit 07b6c67e
"feat(server): operatorPromptSubmit GraphQL mutation with CSRF + idempotency +
identity audit [ddx-f171a715]" and merged to main in 4d6db126.

The previous review BLOCK ("diff contains only an execution result.json
artifact; no schema, resolver, CSRF, idempotency, audit, or test code is
present") inspected an attempt diff that did not actually carry the code. The
code in fact merged and is currently present at HEAD.

Evidence each AC is satisfied
-----------------------------

AC1. operatorPromptSubmit mutation in schema.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:2965 declares the mutation.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go:1261,1271 generated input/result types.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt.go:169
    `(*mutationResolver).OperatorPromptSubmit` is the resolver.

AC2. CSRF required (returns 403 without).
  - resolver_operator_prompt.go:162 errCSRF carries "(status: 403)".
  - resolver_operator_prompt.go:180-189 rejects when the request has no
    *http.Request in ctx, missing header, or token mismatch.
  - StaticCSRFTokenStore.Validate (line 148) is constant-time.
  - Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_CSRF (3 subcases — missing/wrong/valid).

AC3. Idempotency key dedupes within 24h window.
  - OperatorPromptIdempotencyTTL = 24h (resolver_operator_prompt.go:23).
  - MemoryIdempotencyCache.Lookup/Store enforce TTL eviction (lines 92-118).
  - Resolver short-circuits on cache hit returning Deduplicated=true (lines
    197-207) and stores key->beadID after successful create (line 237).
  - Tests: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_Idempotency (same key dedupes, different
    keys do not, no second audit event on dedup); TestMemoryIdempotencyCache_
    TTLEviction (locks 24h boundary at 23h present / 25h evicted).

AC4. Audit event includes identity.
  - resolver_operator_prompt.go:218-235 appends a BeadEvent of kind
    "operator_prompt_submit" whose Body records identity kind, RemoteAddr,
    X-Tailscale-User, X-Tailscale-Node, and idempotency_key, and whose Actor
    is set from operatorPromptIdentity (tsnet user OR localhost:<remote>).
  - Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_HappyPath asserts EventsByKind returns
    exactly one event with non-empty Actor and Body containing identity= and
    idempotency_key=key-1.

AC5. Default = proposed (not ready).
  - bead.NewOperatorPromptBead (internal/bead/types.go:163) sets
    Status = StatusProposed and IssueType = IssueTypeOperatorPrompt.
  - Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_HappyPath asserts Status == StatusProposed
    and IssueType == IssueTypeOperatorPrompt.

AC6. Tests cover all five.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt_test.go has four
    tests (HappyPath, CSRF with 3 subtests, Idempotency, TTLEviction) which
    together exercise AC1–AC5.

Verification
------------

  $ go test ./internal/server/graphql/ -run 'OperatorPrompt|MemoryIdempotency' -v -count=1

Conclusion
----------

No code changes are warranted. The bead's contract is fully met by code
already present at HEAD. Re-running the review against the current tree
(rather than a single attempt's diff) should resolve the BLOCK.

The previous review BLOCK ("diff contains only an execution result.json
artifact; no schema, resolver, CSRF, idempotency, audit, or test code is
present") inspected an attempt diff that did not actually carry the code. The
code in fact merged and is currently present at HEAD.

Evidence each AC is satisfied
-----------------------------

AC1. operatorPromptSubmit mutation in schema.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:2965 declares the mutation.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go:1261,1271 generated input/result types.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt.go:169
    `(*mutationResolver).OperatorPromptSubmit` is the resolver.

AC2. CSRF required (returns 403 without).
  - resolver_operator_prompt.go:162 errCSRF carries "(status: 403)".
  - resolver_operator_prompt.go:180-189 rejects when the request has no
    *http.Request in ctx, missing header, or token mismatch.
  - StaticCSRFTokenStore.Validate (line 148) is constant-time.
  - Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_CSRF (3 subcases — missing/wrong/valid).

AC3. Idempotency key dedupes within 24h window.
  - OperatorPromptIdempotencyTTL = 24h (resolver_operator_prompt.go:23).
  - MemoryIdempotencyCache.Lookup/Store enforce TTL eviction (lines 92-118).
  - Resolver short-circuits on cache hit returning Deduplicated=true (lines
    197-207) and stores key->beadID after successful create (line 237).
  - Tests: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_Idempotency (same key dedupes, different
    keys do not, no second audit event on dedup); TestMemoryIdempotencyCache_
    TTLEviction (locks 24h boundary at 23h present / 25h evicted).

AC4. Audit event includes identity.
  - resolver_operator_prompt.go:218-235 appends a BeadEvent of kind
    "operator_prompt_submit" whose Body records identity kind, RemoteAddr,
    X-Tailscale-User, X-Tailscale-Node, and idempotency_key, and whose Actor
    is set from operatorPromptIdentity (tsnet user OR localhost:<remote>).
  - Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_HappyPath asserts EventsByKind returns
    exactly one event with non-empty Actor and Body containing identity= and
    idempotency_key=key-1.

AC5. Default = proposed (not ready).
  - bead.NewOperatorPromptBead (internal/bead/types.go:163) sets
    Status = StatusProposed and IssueType = IssueTypeOperatorPrompt.
  - Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_HappyPath asserts Status == StatusProposed
    and IssueType == IssueTypeOperatorPrompt.

AC6. Tests cover all five.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt_test.go has four
    tests (HappyPath, CSRF with 3 subtests, Idempotency, TTLEviction) which
    together exercise AC1–AC5.

------------

  PASS
  ok  github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server/graphql 0.028s

Conclusion
----------

No code changes are warranted. The bead's contract is fully met by code
already present at HEAD. Re-running the review against the current tree
(rather than a single attempt's diff) should resolve the BLOCK.

NO CHANGES committed in this attempt.

## Why this bead is currently blocked

The bead description has four parts. Three of the four are blocked on a
prerequisite that has not landed:

(a) "Delete DDx reimplementations of upstream subcommands"
    (agent_list/capabilities/doctor/check/models/providers/route_status/
    catalog/reindex/benchmark/replay)

(c) "Register DDx overrides on mounted subtree (deprecations + condense)"

Both presuppose a mounted upstream Cobra root grafted under `ddx agent`
— i.e. `cli(ddx).agent(load_cli(agent))` per FEAT-001-cli.md:69 and
plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md:28.

The CONTRACT-003 audit (committed 08a40b69) explicitly identifies this
as a gap (docs/helix/02-design/contract-003-audit-2026-04-29.md:23–89):

    > DDx cannot write `agentcli.NewRootCommand()` against the current
    > contract.
    >
    > The plan's "delete with prejudice" list (agent_list,
    > agent_capabilities, agent_doctor, agent_check, agent_models,
    > agent_providers, agent_route_status, agent_catalog,
    > agent_reindex) presupposes that upstream owns these subcommands
    > inside a mountable tree. Today they exist only as flag-driven
    > branches in cmd/agent/main.go that DDx cannot consume
    > programmatically.
    >
    > Plan bead #6 ("open CONTRACT-003 amendment in `~/Projects/agent`
    > if #5 surfaces gaps") is **required, not conditional**, because
    > of this gap.

Concretely:

  * cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (lines 123–141) wires every soon-to-be-deleted
    file via `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentCheckCommand())`,
    `newAgentModelsCommand()`, `newAgentProvidersCommand()`,
    `newAgentRouteStatusCommand()`, `newAgentListCommand()`,
    `newAgentCapabilitiesCommand()`, `newAgentDoctorCommand()`,
    `newAgentBenchmarkCommand()`, `newAgentCatalogCommand()`,
    `newAgentReplayCommand()`. Deleting any of those `agent_*.go`
    files without the upstream `MountCLI` replacement would leave
    agent_cmd.go referencing missing constructors and break the build.
  * The replacement (`cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`) requires
    the upstream module to expose `MountCLI` from a non-`internal/`
    package, which the audit specifies as the upstream amendment
    CONTRACT-003-A (port `cmd/agent/main.go` from `flag` to `cobra`,
    move into `agent/cli`, re-export `MountCLI`, bump module version).
    That work has not happened in `~/Projects/agent`.

(b) "Migrate DDx-only subcommands"
    (agent_executions → tries_*; agent_log → runs_log;
     agent_metrics → runs_metrics; agent_usage folded into runs_metrics;
     agent_workers → work_workers)

This portion is mechanically possible inside DDx alone, but per the
plan (line 208) beads #3, #7, #8, #15 must merge atomically — with #15
landing in isolation, the new namespaces (`ddx runs`, `ddx tries`,
`ddx work workers`) need top-level registration that has no analogue
yet in command_factory_commands.go. The implementation P2 phase of the
plan (lines 196–204) lists "CLI: `ddx runs` / `ddx tries` / `ddx work
workers` namespaces (subsuming migrated subcommands)" as separate work
that comes after the frame/design beads. Doing only (b) here, without
(a) and (c), produces a confusing CLI surface where the same evidence
is reachable both as `ddx agent executions` and `ddx tries list`, and
it strands the deprecation messages that need to point at the new
verbs.

(d) "Update scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to
    new verbs"

This depends on the new verbs (`ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx runs *`,
`ddx tries *`, `ddx work workers *`) actually existing as runnable
commands so that the updated scripts/skills run green. `ddx run` and
`ddx try` are not registered today (grep for `Use:.*\"run\"` /
`Use:.*\"try\"` against cli/cmd/*.go shows only the `ddx agent run`
inner command). Updating scripts/eval-skill.sh,
scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh, scripts/test-doc-snippets.sh,
scripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh,
library/.agents/skills/ddx-refine-plan/SKILL.md, etc. to call
non-existent verbs would break those flows immediately and would
specifically break the in-flight `ddx-refine-plan` skill that produced
this very plan (plan line 208).

## What an unblocked attempt needs

Roughly in dependency order:

1. Upstream amendment CONTRACT-003-A merged in `~/Projects/agent`:
   port to cobra, move commands to `agent/cli`, export `MountCLI`,
   tag a release, and bump `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` in
   DDx's go.mod (no replace directive — see memory
   feedback_no_local_replace).
2. Top-level `ddx run` and `ddx try` registered as first-class
   commands under CommandFactory (the implementation P2 thread of the
   plan).
3. `ddx runs`, `ddx tries`, `ddx work workers` namespace skeletons
   registered, even if their subcommand implementations are then
   ported in this bead.

Once those are in, the present bead becomes:

  * Replace the 11+ enumerated `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgent*Command())`
    calls in agent_cmd.go with `cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`,
    plus deprecation override registrations for `agent run`,
    `agent execute-bead`, `agent execute-loop`, and the override for
    `agent condense` (DDx-specific filter).
  * `git rm` agent_check.go, agent_models.go, agent_providers.go,
    agent_route_status.go (the only "delete with prejudice" files
    that actually exist in DDx today; agent_list/capabilities/doctor/
    catalog/reindex/benchmark/replay only exist as inline functions
    inside agent_cmd.go and would be removed by the agent_cmd.go
    rewrite).
  * Move agent_executions.go → tries_*.go; agent_metrics.go →
    runs_metrics.go (folding agent_usage.go into it);
    agent_workers.go → work_workers.go; rename `newAgentLogCommand`
    in agent_cmd.go into `runs_log.go`. Update tests in lockstep.
  * Sweep scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to
    swap old verbs for new (e.g. `ddx agent run` → `ddx run`,
    `ddx agent execute-loop` → `ddx work`,
    `ddx agent execute-bead` → `ddx try`,
    `ddx agent log/metrics/usage/executions/workers` → the new
    `runs/tries/work workers` paths). Specific known references:
    scripts/eval-skill.sh, scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh,
    scripts/test-doc-snippets.sh, scripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh,
    library/.agents/skills/ddx-refine-plan/SKILL.md,
    library/personas/README.md, cli/cmd/init.go:757,
    cli/cmd/bead_review.go:30–33, plus a wider grep across
    docs/**/*.md and library/**/*.md.

## What is done in this attempt

Nothing on disk — but the verification above (audit cross-reference,
agent_cmd.go wiring inspection, FEAT-001 surface inspection,
search for top-level `run`/`try` registrations) is captured here so
the next attempt can move directly into execution once bead #6 lands.

## Recommended next steps

- Park this bead behind bead #6 (upstream CONTRACT-003-A amendment)
  and the P2 implementation beads that introduce top-level
  `ddx run` / `ddx try` and the `runs`/`tries`/`work workers`
  namespace skeletons.
- Re-attempt with the same id once those prerequisites have merged.
  At that point this bead is mechanical: a contained refactor of
  agent_cmd.go plus a directed grep-and-edit sweep of the call sites
  enumerated above.

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01B6AbPTsfUEXdAmLmB1gcKk","type":"tool_result","content":"---\nddx:\n  id: SD-015-trace\n  depends_on:\n    - SD-015\n    - FEAT-006\n---\n# Resolution Path Trace: CLI Flag → HTTP Request\n\n**Companion doc to** [SD-015 Agent Routing and Catalog Resolution](SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md) and [SD-023 Agent Routing Visibility](SD-023-agent-routing-visibility.md).\n\nSD-015 and SD-023 describe *intent* — what the routing layer should do across\nfive routing modes. This document describes *reality* — how one concrete CLI\ninvocation flows through every layer of the code today, with file:line\ncitations. It exists so a newcomer debugging a routing bug can read one\ndocument instead of reverse-engineering the flow from `routing.go`,\n`discovery.go`, `agent_runner.go`, `runner.go`, and `agent_cmd.go`.\n\n**Author's invocation used throughout this trace:**\n\n```bash\nddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6\n```\n\n**Citations are anchored to base revision `654b4cdcd9200fa8db6c38d6af9dad2acfbaa779` (2026-04-18).**\nLine numbers drift as the code evolves — see the *CI citation check* section\nat the end for how this document is kept in sync.\n\n## Current Gaps (as of 2026-04-18)\n\nEach gap links to its tracking bead. Update this table when a bead closes or a\nnew structural defect is discovered.\n\n| # | Gap | Visible at | Tracking bead |\n|---|-----|------------|---------------|\n| 1 | `RouteRequest` has no `Provider` field; `--provider` is silently dropped during normalization unless it names a registered harness. | `cli/internal/agent/types.go:337` (struct def); `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305` (only consumed as harness alias) | [ddx-8610020e](#) — routing: add Provider field to RouteRequest |\n| 2 | `RunOptions.Provider` is a parallel path consumed by `resolveHarness()` and `resolveNativeAgentProvider()` — bypasses the routing layer entirely for the embedded-agent harness. | `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:382`; `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:519` | [ddx-2f5a2284](#) — ModelRef/Profile paths must consult discovery for `--provider` + catalog override |\n| 3 | `FuzzyMatchModel` does no case-folding and no canonical-prefix normalization. `qwen/qwen3.6` will not match `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit` because the slash, vendor prefix, and case are treated literally. | `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:76` | [ddx-0486e601](#) — fuzzy matcher lacks case + canonical-prefix normalization |\n| 4 | `scoreCandidate` has no provider-affinity bonus: when two harnesses can serve the same discovered model, the scorer does not prefer the harness whose provider actually advertised the model. | `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:477` | [ddx-3c5ba7cc](#) — tier escalation must respect `--provider` affinity |\n| 5 | Fuzzy-match tiebreak picks shortest-suffix, not highest-version. `qwen3` against `[qwen3.5-27b, qwen3.6-35b]` picks `qwen3.5`. | `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:100` | [ddx-0216b966](#) — discovery fuzzy match tiebreak |\n| 6 | No pre-dispatch capability check — context window, tool support, effort, permissions. | `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:396` (`rejectIfNotViable` covers availability only) | [ddx-4817edfd](#) — pre-dispatch capability check |\n\n## Sequence Diagram\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    actor User\n    participant CLI as cli/cmd/work.go + agent_cmd.go\n    participant Norm as NormalizeRouteRequest<br/>(routing.go:289)\n    participant Disc as DiscoverProviderModels<br/>(discovery.go:124)\n    participant Plan as BuildCandidatePlans<br/>(routing.go:27)\n    participant Fuzzy as FuzzyMatchModel<br/>(discovery.go:66)\n    participant Score as scoreCandidate<br/>(routing.go:477)\n    participant Sel as SelectBestCandidate<br/>(routing.go:556)\n    participant Run as Runner.Run<br/>(runner.go:141)\n    participant RunAgent as Runner.RunAgent<br/>(agent_runner.go:65)\n    participant Resolve as resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider<br/>(agent_runner.go:464)\n    participant AgentLib as agentlib.Run<br/>(agent_runner.go:320)\n    participant HTTP as Provider HTTP endpoint\n\n    User->>CLI: ddx work --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6\n    CLI->>Norm: RouteFlags{...}\n    Note over Norm: --provider consumed only<br/>if it names a registered harness<br/>(GAP #1)\n    Norm-->>CLI: RouteRequest{ModelPin, Profile}\n    CLI->>Disc: DiscoverProviderModels(workDir, timeout)\n    Note over Disc: 30s process-wide cache\n    Disc-->>CLI: DiscoveryResult{Providers[]}\n    CLI->>Plan: BuildCandidatePlans(req, states)\n    loop each harness\n        Plan->>Plan: evaluateCandidate\n        Plan->>Plan: rejectIfNotViable\n    end\n    Plan->>Fuzzy: FuzzyMatchModel (when uncataloged)\n    Fuzzy-->>Plan: resolved model ID\n    Plan->>Score: scoreCandidate per plan\n    Plan-->>Sel: []CandidatePlan (ranked)\n    Sel-->>CLI: best plan\n    CLI->>Run: Runner.Run(RunOptions)\n    Run->>RunAgent: if harnessName == \"agent\"\n    RunAgent->>Resolve: resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider(wd, model, provider, modelRef)\n    Resolve->>Disc: second-pass lookup (cached)\n    Resolve-->>RunAgent: {Provider, BaseURL, APIKey, Model}\n    RunAgent->>AgentLib: agentlib.Run(ctx, Request{...})\n    AgentLib->>HTTP: POST /v1/chat/completions\n    HTTP-->>AgentLib: stream | unexpected EOF\n    AgentLib-->>RunAgent: Result\n    RunAgent-->>User: Result (stdout + session log)\n```\n\n## Layer 1 — CLI flag parse\n\n`ddx work` is a thin alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`. All flags are cloned\nfrom the inner command.\n\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:10` — `newWorkCommand` constructs the alias.\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:40` — flags are cloned verbatim from `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand`.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1319` — `--harness` flag declaration.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1320` — `--model` flag declaration.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1321` — `--provider` flag declaration.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1322` — `--model-ref` flag declaration.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1327` — `--local` flag declaration.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1338` — `runAgentExecuteLoop` entry point reads flags.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1342` through `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1357` — flag values\n  are plucked into local variables (`harness`, `model`, `provider`, `modelRef`,\n  `effort`, `local`).\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1374` — `--local` branch takes the inline path; without\n  it the loop submits to the running DDx server.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1384` — `ValidateForExecuteLoop(harness, model, provider, modelRef)`\n  runs before any bead is claimed.\n\nThe flag values eventually reach `ExecuteBead`, which in turn builds\n`RunOptions`:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:62` — `RunOptions` struct definition.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:498` — `runner.Run(RunOptions{...})`\n  constructs the options, including `Provider: opts.Provider` on line 504.\n\n**Observation:** `RouteFlags` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:51`) has a `Provider` field; so does\n`RunOptions` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:74`). They are the raw CLI inputs. The gap is not here\n— it is at the normalization boundary (Layer 2).\n\n## Layer 2 — Normalize flags into `RouteRequest`\n\nThe normalization boundary converts raw CLI flags into the minimal routing ask\nthat the routing engine evaluates.\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:289` — `NormalizeRouteRequest(flags RouteFlags, cfg Config, catalog *Catalog) RouteRequest`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:294` — `RouteRequest{Effort, Permissions}`\n  initial struct. Note the absence of `Provider`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:303` — `flags.Harness != \"\"` branch assigns\n  `HarnessOverride` directly.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305` — `flags.Provider != \"\"` branch: the\n  provider flag is **only** consulted as a harness-alias lookup. If\n  `--provider vidar-omlx` does not match any entry in `builtinHarnesses`, the\n  value is silently discarded.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:318` — `flags.Model != \"\"` branch routes the\n  model through `catalog.NormalizeModelRef` to produce either `ModelRef` (known\n  in catalog) or `ModelPin` (exact, bypasses catalog policy).\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:337` — `RouteRequest` struct definition. Fields:\n  `Profile`, `ModelRef`, `ModelPin`, `Effort`, `Permissions`, `HarnessOverride`.\n  **No `Provider` field — see Gap #1.**\n\n**Concrete outcome for our invocation:**\n`--provider vidar-omlx` does not name a registered harness, so the provider\nflag is dropped at `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305`. `--model-ref qwen/qwen3.6` flows through\n`NormalizeModelRef`; since it is not in the shared catalog, it becomes a\n`ModelPin`. The resulting `RouteRequest` carries only\n`{ModelPin: \"qwen/qwen3.6\"}`.\n\n## Layer 3 — Discovery probe\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:117` — `init()` sets the process-wide cache\n  TTL to `30 * time.Second`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:124` — `DiscoverProviderModels(workDir, timeout)` entry.\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:125` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:130` — cache hit returns the cached result if still fresh.\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:141` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:155` — probes every configured provider via `providerstatus.Probe`; When invoked with\n# --strict, also verifies the line contains a Go symbol hint when the path\n# looks like Go source.\n#\n# Cited as the CI step that prevents SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md from\n# rotting silently as routing.go / discovery.go / types.go evolve.\n\nset -euo pipefail\n\nDOC_PATH=\"${1:-docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md}\"\n\nif [ ! -f \"$DOC_PATH\" ]; then\n    echo \"citations: $DOC_PATH not found\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\n# Match citations of the form `path/to/file.ext:NN` inside backticks.\n# We only validate citations whose path looks like a file (contains a slash\n# and ends with a known extension).\ncitations=$(grep -oE '`[A-Za-z0-9_./-]+\\.(go|ts|tsx|js|yaml|yml|md|sh):[0-9]+`' \"$DOC_PATH\" | tr -d '`' | sort -u)\n\nif [ -z \"$citations\" ]; then\n    echo \"citations: no citations found in $DOC_PATH — fail-closed\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\nfailures=0\ntotal=0\n\nwhile IFS= read -r cite; do\n    [ -z \"$cite\" ] && continue\n    total=$((total + 1))\n    path=\"${cite%:*}\"\n    line=\"${cite##*:}\"\n    if [ ! -f \"$path\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: MISSING FILE: $cite\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\n    # Line count check; awk 'END{print NR}' counts lines portably.\n    nlines=$(awk 'END{print NR}' \"$path\")\n    if [ \"$line\" -gt \"$nlines\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: LINE OUT OF RANGE: $cite (file has $nlines lines)\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\n    # Sanity check: the line must not be blank, since every real citation\n    # points at a struct, func, or statement.\n    content=$(awk -v n=\"$line\" 'NR==n' \"$path\")\n    if [ -z \"$(echo \"$content\" | tr -d '[:space:]')\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: BLANK LINE: $cite\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\ndone <<< \"$citations\"\n\nif [ \"$failures\" -gt 0 ]; then\n    echo \"citations: $failures/$total failed for $DOC_PATH\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\necho \"citations: $total/$total ok for $DOC_PATH\"","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d932d409-7007-4d29-af52-a78f704e35c0","uuid":"465c02eb-3d0f-4e0e-9149-057b595f6ab5","timestamp":"2026-04-18T06:48:07.386Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"#!/usr/bin/env bash\n# Verifies every `path:line` citation in SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md\n# points to a file that exists and a line that exists. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GZzJDTKtx6XSP7gKPQq28S","type":"tool_result","content":"282\t//     harness binary receives the model string as-is and can error clearly.\n283\t//     - Otherwise: try catalog resolution:\n284\t//     - If known in catalog: ModelRef.\n285\t//     - If not known: ModelPin (exact pin, bypasses catalog).\n286\t//  3. If flags.Profile is set → Profile.\n287\t//  4. Else fall back to cfg.Model (same catalog resolution) or cfg.Profile.\n288\t//  5. If nothing is set → cfg.Harness becomes HarnessOverride (legacy fallback).\n289\tfunc NormalizeRouteRequest(flags RouteFlags, cfg Config, catalog *Catalog) RouteRequest {\n290\t\tif catalog == nil {\n291\t\t\tcatalog = BuiltinCatalog\n292\t\t}\n293\t\n294\t\treq := RouteRequest{\n295\t\t\tEffort:      flags.Effort,\n296\t\t\tPermissions: flags.Permissions,\n297\t\t}\n298\t\n299\t\t// 1. Harness override constrains routing to one harness.\n300\t\t//    --provider that names a known harness (or alias like \"local\") also\n301\t\t//    pins the harness so the routing engine never falls through to a\n302\t\t//    different harness.\n303\t\tif flags.Harness != \"\" {\n304\t\t\treq.HarnessOverride = flags.Harness\n305\t\t} else if flags.Provider != \"\" {\n306\t\t\tresolved := resolveHarnessAlias(flags.Provider)\n307\t\t\t// Only treat as harness override if it names a registered harness;\n308\t\t\t// otherwise it's a ddx-agent provider name (e.g. \"vidar\") and\n309\t\t\t// should not constrain harness selection.\n310\t\t\tif _, ok := builtinHarnesses[resolved]; ok {\n311\t\t\t\treq.HarnessOverride = resolved"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01BpB5ybRXLsC8V3pvMEmJe9","session_id":"d932d409-7007-4d29-af52-a78f704e35c0","uuid":"3708917d-10f5-4047-87a8-eca15bf792b1","timestamp":"2026-04-18T06:48:52.477Z"}

Returns\n65\t// empty string if no match found.\n66\tfunc (d *DiscoveryResult) FuzzyMatchModel(input string) (resolved string, fuzzy bool) {\n67\t\tif d == nil {\n68\t\t\treturn \"\", false\n69\t\t}\n70\t\treturn fuzzyMatchInPool(input, d.AllModels())\n71\t}\n72\t\n73\t// fuzzyMatchInPool resolves input against a pool of model IDs."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01BpB5ybRXLsC8V3pvMEmJe9","session_id":"d932d409-7007-4d29-af52-a78f704e35c0","uuid":"21b0e478-edd0-4d23-81e6-41a9cd63003c","timestamp":"2026-04-18T06:48:53.316Z"}

rationale: Bead ddx-ce7a796e (B22d-g) cannot be landed in isolation against base
41d464b03c4e5d12b7a9244c3c59ee312e64e255. Same fundamental blocker as
B22d-f (ddx-9f4b1a8c, no_changes 2026-04-27): lefthook `runtime-lint`
hook (lefthook.yml) filters analyzer diagnostics to the set of staged
files, and the in-scope test files contain pre-existing RunOptions /
CompareOptions composite literals whose underlying APIs are explicitly
named out-of-scope by this bead's parent decomposition (step h owns
service_run.go; runner.go and claude_stream.go aren't in this step's
in-scope at all).

What is done (verified locally, then reverted)
- compare_adapter.go is straightforward to drain of RunOptions /
  CompareOptions / QuorumOptions:
    * RunFunc redeclared as `func(rcfg config.ResolvedConfig,
      runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error)`.
    * defaultResolvePromptForCompare(runtime AgentRunRuntime).
    * runCompareArmWith(run RunFunc, rcfg, runtime CompareRuntime,
      armIdx, harnessName, baseDir, prompt, worktreePath).
    * RunCompareWith(run, rcfg, runtime CompareRuntime, resolvePrompt,
      cleanupSandbox).
    * RunQuorumWith(run, rcfg, runtime QuorumRuntime).
    * RunBenchmarkWith(runCompare func(config.ResolvedConfig,
      CompareRuntime) (*ComparisonRecord, error), suite).
    * RunCompareViaService / RunCompareWithAgent / RunQuorumViaService /
      RunQuorumWithAgent / RunBenchmarkViaService / RunBenchmarkWithAgent
      rewritten to take (rcfg, runtime) instead of CompareOptions /
      QuorumOptions and to delegate through the new RunFunc.
    * RunCompareWithConfigViaService / RunQuorumWithConfigViaService
      collapsed into a single forwarding hop (they no longer need to
      assemble a CompareOptions/QuorumOptions intermediary).
    * RunQuorumWithConfig retained, now calling RunQuorumWith with the
      new shape.
- models.go BenchmarkArmsToCompare changes return shape from
  CompareOptions to CompareRuntime (and accepts an AgentRunRuntime
  baseline rather than a RunOptions). compare_adapter.go's
  RunBenchmarkWith uses that shape directly.
- models_test.go updates the BenchmarkArmsToCompare call to pass
  `AgentRunRuntime{Prompt: "test"}` and assert
  CompareRuntime.{Harnesses, ArmModels, ArmLabels}. This is the only
  RunOptions-related drift in models_test.go — the rest of that file
  doesn't reference RunOptions.
- After the in-package edits, `cd cli && go build ./...` passes and
  `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` is clean once the test
  files in the next bullet are also updated.
- agent_test.go (out of scope) needs one closure-shape update at
  line 584 because RunFunc's signature changed; with that one-line
  diff applied locally, TestQuorumRunsAllHarnesses passes.

What is blocking (lint policy vs. bead scope)

1. claude_stream_test.go (in-scope) contains RunOptions composite
   literals at lines 206, 212, 218, 224, 291, and 324. These are all
   inside test cases that call helpers declared in claude_stream.go —
   resolveClaudeProgressLogDir(opts RunOptions, cfg Config) at
   claude_stream.go:284 and runClaudeStreamingFn(r, ctx, harness,
   "claude", "", opts RunOptions, "hi", "", 10*time.Second) at
   claude_stream.go:304/477/507. claude_stream.go is NOT in this
   bead's in-scope; B22d's per-step decomposition reserves it for a
   separate step. With claude_stream.go's RunOptions surface
   unchanged, the test cannot construct anything other than
   RunOptions{...} to drive resolveClaudeProgressLogDir, and the
   runtime-lint hook flags every one of those literals when the
   file is staged.

2. prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go (in-scope) has three RunOptions
   composite literals:
     * line 78  — r.resolvePrompt(RunOptions{PromptFile: fixture})
                  drives Runner.resolvePrompt in runner.go:976.
                  runner.go is out of scope.
     * line 85  — defaultResolvePromptForCompare(RunOptions{...})
                  drives the in-scope helper. CAN be updated.
     * line 112 — RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})
                  drives service_run.go:104. service_run.go's public
                  API is explicitly named out-of-scope ("step h").
   Even after migrating line 85 and updating line 98's RunBenchmarkWith
   closure to the new (rcfg, runtime CompareRuntime) shape, the file
   still carries lines 78 and 112 as pre-existing RunOptions
   composite literals. runtime-lint scopes diagnostics by file (not
   by hunk), so the staged file fails the gate on lines that this
   bead is not authorized to touch.

3. agent_test.go (out of scope, but transitively required) — once
   RunFunc's signature changes, agent_test.go:584's
   `func(opts RunOptions) (*Result, error)` no longer matches
   RunQuorumWithConfig's RunFunc parameter, so the package fails to
   build. The minimal fix is one line, but agent_test.go has 18
   other RunOptions composite literals (BuildArgs call sites at
   lines 118, 130, 148, 157, 165, 188, 196, 209, 214, 677, 684,
   718, 727, 856, 864, 971, plus 678, 684) that are pre-existing,
   unrelated to this bead, and depend on BuildArgs (runner.go) being
   migrated separately. Staging agent_test.go would surface every
   one of those as a lint violation.

The runtime-lint hook is mandatory (per project policy: --no-verify is
forbidden). Diagnostic filtering is whole-file, not hunk-aware, so
"only edit my own lines" doesn't help when the file already contained
violations that this bead's named scope can't reach.

What a follow-up attempt would need (any of the following)

A. Re-decompose B22d-g to also include claude_stream.go (so
   resolveClaudeProgressLogDir / runClaudeStreamingFn migrate off
   RunOptions in the same commit, draining claude_stream_test.go),
   AND service_run.go's RunViaServiceWith + runner.go's
   resolvePrompt (so prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go drains), AND
   agent_test.go's BuildArgs call sites (so the RunFunc signature
   change compiles cleanly across the package). This is essentially
   the previously-attempted atomic B22d scope (worker-3f7a's
   recommendation, applied 2026-04-26 in commit 97561a6e); the
   subsequent re-decomposition into steps a-h reintroduced this
   exact tension.

B. Land step h FIRST (and in fact most of the rest of the
   per-file decomposition) so that claude_stream.go, runner.go,
   service_run.go, and agent_test.go no longer contain pre-existing
   RunOptions usages, then run step g — at that point the in-scope
   test files have no out-of-scope violations and lint passes on the
   staged set. The current bead-queue ordering puts step g before
   step h, so this requires a tracker reordering.

C. Change runtime-lint's filter from whole-file to hunk-scoped (so
   pre-existing violations on lines this bead doesn't touch don't
   block the commit). This is a lint-policy change in lefthook.yml;
   not in this bead's scope and not authorized by the bead
   description.

D. Authorize the worker (model-side) to extend in-scope to cover
   the transitively-required files (claude_stream.go,
   runner.go.resolvePrompt + BuildArgs, service_run.go.RunViaServiceWith,
   and agent_test.go), the same way B22d itself was authorized in
   2026-04-26 (commit 97561a6e). The diff for that expansion is
   already prepared and verified locally; only the bead-scope
   constraint stands in the way.

The implementation is mechanical and verified locally; the blocker is
project-level lint policy versus per-step bead scope, not
implementation difficulty. No partial commit is justified, since
landing only the in-scope-clean half (compare_adapter.go +
models.go + models_test.go) breaks the package build by leaving
agent_test.go with an incompatible RunFunc closure, and updating
that closure trips the lint on agent_test.go's pre-existing 18+
RunOptions literals.

Updated\n    ddx-b808df39 notes to cross-reference this investigation so the\n    operator stop/kill tool tracks the underlying fixes.\n\ndiff --git a/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md b/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..5e8547cf\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,193 @@\n+# Investigation: root cause of 33–142h hung execute-bead workers\n+\n+**Bead:** ddx-0a651925 (investigates; sibling to ddx-b808df39)\n+**Attempt:** 20260418T043148-1346e8a3\n+**Scope:** Source-code + execution-bundle analysis. No live hung worker was available\n+in this isolated worktree to capture live `ps`/`pprof`, so the incident evidence is\n+drawn from `.ddx/executions/*` bundles and the worker/runner source path.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 1. Observed evidence (from `.ddx/executions/`)\n+\n+| Metric                                               | Count / value                                        |\n+| ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |\n+| Total attempt bundles                                | 328                                                  |\n+| Bundles with `result.json`                           | 291 (89%)                                            |\n+| Bundles with `manifest.json` but **no** `result.json` | 37 (11%) — executions whose worker never finalized  |\n+\n+Top five longest *finalized* attempts:\n+\n+| `duration_ms` | Wall time    | `detail`                                                                    | Bundle                          |\n+| ------------- | ------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |\n+| 7,720,908     | **2h 8m**    | `agent: compaction stuck: 50 consecutive failed compaction attempts`        | 20260416T230316-efbf91c5        |\n+| 7,200,098     | **2h 0m**    | `timeout after 2h0m0s` (claude streaming wall-clock fired)                  | 20260415T015231-c2ffa9ba        |\n+| 6,764,927     | **1h 53m**   | success (agent harness, qwen)                                               | 20260416T230315-f9f46822        |\n+| 5,649,987     | **1h 34m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260416T230313-e193942b        |\n+| 4,284,347     | **1h 11m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260415T103422-bb3a45fa        |\n+\n+**Key observation:** the 33h / 142h hang durations cited in ddx-b808df39 are\n+~15× and ~65× longer than the longest *finalized* attempt on record. This means\n+the hung workers never reached the point of writing `result.json` — they were\n+blocked inside `runner.Run` (or deeper) for days. Every code path that\n+*should* have terminated them (idle timeout, compaction-stuck, stall detector,\n+cancel propagation) failed to fire.\n+\n+The 37 manifest-only bundles are candidates for hung or externally-killed\n+workers; e.g. `20260416T215953-6b515c05` has a manifest from 2026-04-16T21:59Z\n+with no result. Those fit the profile of \"workers running 33h–142h with no bead\n+assigned\" reported on agent projects.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 2. Root-cause classification (source code evidence)\n+\n+The investigation checked the four hypotheses from the bead description.\n+\n+### RC1 (PRIMARY) — Caller context is discarded inside the Runner\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:239` (`Runner.Run`):\n+```go\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n+```\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:163` (`Runner.RunAgent`):\n+```go\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n+```\n+\n+`RunOptions` (types.go) takes no `context.Context` at all. `agent.ExecuteBead`\n+likewise does not accept ctx, and `server.WorkerManager.runWorker` passes its\n+own ctx into `worker.Run(ctx, …)` but that ctx **never reaches the agent\n+invocation**.\n+\n+**Impact:** `WorkerManager.Stop(id)` (workers.go:648) calls `handle.cancel()`\n+which cancels only the worker goroutine's ctx. The running agent provider call\n+/ subprocess is detached. The only way to interrupt a stuck agent today is a\n+SIGKILL from outside the process. This is the structural defect that enables\n+33–142h hangs — once the agent is stuck, nothing inside DDx can stop it.\n+\n+### RC2 — Idle-reset timer, not wall-clock deadline\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/executor.go:35-40` creates an *idle timeout* context value:\n+```go\n+func withExecutionTimeout(ctx context.Context, timeout time.Duration) context.Context {\n+    return context.WithValue(ctx, executionTimeoutKey{}, timeout)\n+}\n+```\n+\n+`executor.go:154-178` consumes it and **resets the timer on every stdout/stderr\n+byte**. `agent_runner.go:264-285` implements the same pattern against agentlib\n+events.\n+\n+The default timeout is 2h (`types.go:363 DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000`). The\n+comment claims \"2 hours — long enough for any task; supervisor/agent decides\n+termination\" but the actual behaviour is **\"2 hours of silence required to fire\"**.\n+A provider that emits a heartbeat, a ping, a retry line, or any byte resets the\n+timer indefinitely.\n+\n+The `runClaudeStreaming` path (`claude_stream.go:373-384`) is the only path that\n+uses a wall-clock deadline — and it is the only path we see actually hitting\n+the timeout (bundle 20260415T015231 exactly 2h 0m 0.098s).\n+\n+**Impact:** for `harness=agent` (qwen, minimax, openrouter, etc.), no wall-clock\n+bound exists. 33–142h hangs are consistent with a provider that dribbles events\n+slowly enough to defeat the idle reset.\n+\n+### RC3 — Compaction-stuck breaker fires late\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:218-241` increments a counter on each no-op\n+compaction event and trips at `stuckThreshold = maxConsecutiveCompactionFailures`\n+(50 by default). On observed `efbf91c5` it took **2h 8m** to trip. If one\n+iteration takes 3 minutes to produce a no-op compaction event, 50 events =\n+~150 min — matches the observed tail.\n+\n+**Impact:** on its own, the breaker is too slow to be a useful hang-guard. It\n+should be replaced or complemented by a wall-clock-bounded variant.\n+\n+### RC4 — Provider HTTP requests likely lack a per-request deadline\n+\n+`RunAgent` passes the internal `context.Background()`-derived ctx into\n+`agentlib.Run(ctx, req)`. The agent library's providers (`openai.Client`,\n+anthropic client) do not appear to install a per-request deadline themselves\n+(grepping the v0.3.14 module cache shows short timeouts on discovery/probe\n+only; the main call path has no WithTimeout). A stalled TCP socket after\n+headers are received but before the response body arrives can hang a streaming\n+read indefinitely.\n+\n+**Impact:** consistent with \"alive but mid-LLM-call with no client-side\n+deadline\" — hypothesis (2) in the bead description. clean base before this attempt.

The fix itself is in cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1796–1840 — the executor
closure passed to ExecuteBeadWorker captures the worker-level `harness`,
`model`, `provider`, `modelRef`, and `effort` from the surrounding
Cobra-flag scope and rebuilds an attemptRcfg from them on every call.
Because Go closures capture by reference, every retry iteration reads
the same pin values the worker was launched with. The mirror wiring is
in cli/internal/server/workers.go.

that would advance the AC. Adding speculative code or duplicate tests
would violate DOWITYTD.

layer so the post-merge review can produce a verdict and close the bead,
or (b) the bead is force-closed with a note pointing at the existing
test file and production wiring above. Neither is execute-loop work.

Once typed intake exists, safe bead improvement needs its own narrow implementation slice. Without this, the system can identify a fixable bead but cannot improve it before an implementer claims it.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:680 currently records lint results but does not mutate bead description/acceptance before claim.
- cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:110 builds a lint prompt, not a safe rewrite contract with intent-preserving constraints.

PROPOSED FIX
- Handle actionable_but_rewritten by applying only safe, intent-preserving description/AC updates before Claim.
- Record a triage-rewritten/intake-rewritten event with before/after metadata and rationale.
- Reject unsafe rewrites that invent behavior, choose product semantics, expand scope, or remove acceptance criteria; route those to ambiguous_needs_human.

NON-SCOPE
- Decomposition/child creation.
- Depth cap handling.",

.ddx/attachments/ddx-0f3ae192/events.jsonl:9:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Epic bead ddx-0f3ae192 (SD-024: Config-Driven Runtime Options) — no code diff required.

What is done
------------
All four acceptance criteria are satisfied by code already merged on the base
branch (cfc89b73) via the 35 closed child beads. Verified in this worktree:

1. All 35 child stage beads closed.
   `ddx bead list --label sd-024` shows every sd-024 bead in `closed` state
   except this epic itself.

2. ExecuteBeadLoopOptions / RunOptions / ExecuteBeadOptions / CompareOptions /
   QuorumOptions no longer exist as types in cli/internal/agent/.
   `grep -rn "ExecuteBeadLoopOptions\b|^type RunOptions|^type ExecuteBeadOptions|
   ^type CompareOptions|^type QuorumOptions" --include="*.go" cli/internal/agent/`
   returns zero matches.

3. config.LoadAndResolve usable from all three dispatch sites:
   - CLI `ddx work` / agent run / quorum / compare / execute-bead — `cmd/agent_cmd.go`
     lines 187, 245, 348, 1243, 1463, 1743, 2034 + `cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:119`.
   - Server `runWorker` — `internal/server/workers.go:897` (also :586 for retries).
   - GraphQL `StartWorker` — `internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:103`.

4. runtimelint analyzer integrated into Lefthook + CI and passes clean.
   - Source: `tools/lint/runtimelint/` and `tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint`.
   - Lefthook: `lefthook.yml:171` (pre-commit, staged-package scope) and
     `lefthook.yml:441` (full-tree gate).
   - Run: `go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint ./...` exits 0
     with no output against the current tree.

Why there is nothing to commit
------------------------------
SD-024 is an epic; its implementation lives in the 35 child stage beads
(stages 1–4: Foundation, RunOptions migration, ExecuteBeadOptions migration,
Lint enforcement + test debt cleanup). Each child bead landed its own commit
on prior execute-bead runs and the work is already on the base revision this
worktree is rooted at. The epic bead is purely a roll-up — closing it does
not require new code, and committing execution-evidence-only would (and on
the previous attempt did) trip post-merge review with REVIEW:BLOCK because
no implementation diff appears against the epic.

What a follow-up would need
---------------------------
Nothing in this worktree. The orchestrator should mark ddx-0f3ae192 closed
on the strength of the closed child beads + the AC verification above. If
post-merge review re-flags the epic for "no diff", the correct resolution is
to close the epic administratively rather than re-execute it as an
implementation bead.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-ee2cd434/events.jsonl:12:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes_rationale — bead ddx-ee2cd434 (SD-024 Stage 2)

(a) WHAT IS DONE
----------------
The previous attempt (commit 5ae02964) migrated the high-value `r.Run(RunOptions{...})`
and `RunViaService(..., RunOptions{...})` test sites onto the SD-024 successor API
(`r.RunWithConfig` + `RunWithConfigViaService` + `AgentRunRuntime`) across:

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go    (~22 sites: TestRunWith*, TestSessionLogging,
                                         TestRunPromptFile, TestRunStdinMode,
                                         TestRunModel*, TestRunNonZeroExit,
                                         TestRunUnknownHarness, TestRunEmptyPrompt,
                                         TestIntegration_CodexEcho, TestRunOpencode*,
                                         and the *Permissions* pair)
  - cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go   (lmstudio / openrouter dispatch regressions)
  - cli/internal/agent/script_test.go   (runScript helper now builds rcfg via
                                         TestRunConfigOpts and dispatches via
                                         RunWithConfigViaService)

Those 26 sites are the bulk of the "test sites that exercise the high-level agent
run path" called out in the bead description.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING
--------------------
The bead AC reads:

    rg 'RunOptions\{|CompareOptions\{|QuorumOptions\{' cli/ returns 0 matches outside
    the type definitions about to be retired.

This is a *global* gate. It cannot be satisfied by test-only changes — it requires
retiring every remaining `RunOptions{...}` construction in both production and test
code. The remaining sites fall into two classes:

CLASS A — tests that call functions whose signature *takes* RunOptions, so the
RunOptions construction is not a "test site" the test could rewrite without
changing the function under test:

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go        BuildArgs(h, RunOptions{...}, model)  — 14 sites
                                            BuildArgs is the function under test.
  - cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go
        line 78  resolvePrompt(RunOptions{PromptFile})              — exercises r.resolvePrompt
        line 85  defaultResolvePromptForCompare(RunOptions{...})    — exercises pkg-level helper
        line 112 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})  — exercises stub-injection helper
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go
        line 195 SessionIndexEntryFromResult(root, RunOptions{...}, ...)
        line 278 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/models_test.go
        line 45  BenchmarkArmsToCompare(arms, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/virtual_test.go
        lines 164/174/214 runVirtualFn(runner, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go
        lines 206/212/218/224/291/324  internal opts: RunOptions{...} fixtures

Migrating any of these test sites requires first changing the *signature* of the
production function under test (BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare,
RunViaServiceWith, runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, SessionIndexEntryFromResult,
plus the internal claude_stream helpers). That is production-code refactor work,
explicitly outside the scope the bead description names.

CLASS B — production callers still constructing RunOptions{...}:

  - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go              :1462
  - cli/internal/server/server.go     :1913, :3786
  - cli/internal/exec/store.go        :201
  - cli/internal/agent/runner.go      :248, :281, :300, :321, :976
  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go :82  (RunWithConfigViaService bridge — by design)
  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go :61, :261, :414, :685
  - cli/internal/agent/grade.go       :169
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go :706
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go :531

Until these are migrated (and in many cases until the function signatures they
flow through are migrated), the AC's grep gate cannot return zero matches.

The fresh-eyes review on the prior attempt itself concluded:

    Path forward: simplest fix is splitting follow-up beads for
    (a) prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,
    (b) remaining *_test.go RunOptions sites,
    (c) production-code migration,
    then closing this bead with a narrower AC.

That assessment is correct. The bead title ("5-10 sites") and description ("migrate
test sites … in agent_test.go and any sibling test using RunOptions") describe a
test-scoped refactor; the AC describes a global retirement. The two are not
compatible inside one bead.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT WOULD NEED
---------------------------------------
Either:

  Option 1 (preferred): re-cut this bead with a narrowed AC, e.g.

      "rg 'RunOptions\{' cli/internal/agent/{agent,runner,script}_test.go returns
      0 matches in tests that exercise r.Run / RunViaService."

      That AC matches the test-only scope, and the prior commit already satisfies it.

  Option 2: expand bead scope to cover the production migration as well. That is
      a multi-file Stage-2 refactor touching cmd/, internal/server/, internal/exec/,
      and most of internal/agent/. It also requires migrating the signatures of
      BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare, RunViaServiceWith,
      runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, and SessionIndexEntryFromResult so the
      test sites in Class A can shed RunOptions. This belongs in its own bead (or
      epic), not in a "5-10 sites" test-migration bead.

No new code is committed in this attempt because:

  1. The work the bead description actually scopes is already done (5ae02964).
  2. Doing test-only edits that don't move the AC gate would be exploratory churn.
  3. Doing the full production migration here would silently expand the bead scope
     past what the description and title authorize.

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go        BuildArgs(h, RunOptions{...}, model)  — 14 sites
                                            BuildArgs is the function under test.
  - cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go
        line 78  resolvePrompt(RunOptions{PromptFile})              — exercises r.resolvePrompt
        line 85  defaultResolvePromptForCompare(RunOptions{...})    — exercises pkg-level helper
        line 112 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})  — exercises stub-injection helper
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go
        line 195 SessionIndexEntryFromResult(root, RunOptions{...}, ...)
        line 278 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/models_test.go
        line 45  BenchmarkArmsToCompare(arms, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/virtual_test.go
        lines 164/174/214 runVirtualFn(runner, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go
        lines 206/212/218/224/291/324  internal opts: RunOptions{...} fixtures

Migrating any of these test sites requires first changing the *signature* of the
production function under test (BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare,
RunViaServiceWith, runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, SessionIndexEntryFromResult,
plus the internal claude_stream helpers). That is production-code refactor work,
explicitly outside the scope the bead description names.

CLASS B — production callers still constructing RunOptions{...}:

  - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go              :1462
  - cli/internal/server/server.go     :1913, :3786
  - cli/internal/exec/store.go        :201
  - cli/internal/agent/runner.go      :248, :281, :300, :321, :976
  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go :82  (RunWithConfigViaService bridge — by design)
  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go :61, :261, :414, :685
  - cli/internal/agent/grade.go       :169
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go :706
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go :531

Until these are migrated (and in many cases until the function signatures they
flow through are migrated), the AC's grep gate cannot return zero matches.

The fresh-eyes review on the prior attempt itself concluded:

    Path forward: simplest fix is splitting follow-up beads for
    (a) prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,
    (b) remaining *_test.go RunOptions sites,
    (c) production-code migration,
    then closing this bead with a narrower AC.

That assessment is correct. The bead title ("5-10 sites") and description ("migrate
test sites … in agent_test.go and any sibling test using RunOptions") describe a
test-scoped refactor; the AC describes a global retirement. The two are not
compatible inside one bead.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT WOULD NEED
---------------------------------------
Either:

  Option 1 (preferred): re-cut this bead with a narrowed AC, e.g.

      "rg 'RunOptions\{' cli/internal/agent/{agent,runner,script}_test.go returns
      0 matches in tests that exercise r.Run / RunViaService."

      That AC matches the test-only scope, and the prior commit already satisfies it.

  Option 2: expand bead scope to cover the production migration as well. That is
      a multi-file Stage-2 refactor touching cmd/, internal/server/, internal/exec/,
      and most of internal/agent/. It also requires migrating the signatures of
      BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare, RunViaServiceWith,
      runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, and SessionIndexEntryFromResult so the
      test sites in Class A can shed RunOptions. This belongs in its own bead (or
      epic), not in a "5-10 sites" test-migration bead.

No new code is committed in this attempt because:

  1. The work the bead description actually scopes is already done (5ae02964).
  2. Doing test-only edits that don't move the AC gate would be exploratory churn.
  3. Doing the full production migration here would silently expand the bead scope
     past what the description and title authorize.
